Absolute Sensei - The "Demon" Teacher

Concept: Chef Fuukari
Artist: Blade_1605
Plot:
> Like Sensei in the original, he always dedicates all his love and tries to protect his beloved students... but in a much more extreme way, as he abuses the adult card to the point where his body gradually mutates like the Gematria.
> His lifespan is only 7 years left, because in this timeline, Hoshino is not as lucky as in the original and is not rescued in time... when the group arrives, she is just a cold corpse with her chest devoured by crows, forcing him to sacrifice almost his entire lifespan to revive Hoshino using the adult card, creating a new body for her using his own life force for her soul to enter. (As for him... he had to consume Hoshino's old body like Denji consumed the seven-course Makima to transfer all the side effects of reincarnation to his own body, otherwise, sooner or later, Hoshino's new body would gradually rot away.)
> In terms of strength, he has physical strength equivalent to a Decagramatton thanks to a secret contract (in exchange for that, his Shittim Chest is much weaker than the original, as it cannot automatically calculate bullet trajectories to deflect or create shields, and its battery life is pitifully low). Although he can only maintain that strength for a few minutes and cannot withstand bullets, he has enough reflexes and speed to dodge all the bullets from Arius's army, almost cornering Saori before getting shot himself while trying to protect other students from halo-destroying bombs.

Hundreds of chapters of build up

||Absolute Hoshino - The Girl Filled with Suffering
Concept: Chef Fuukari
Illustration: Blade_1605
Plot:
> Unlike the original Hoshino, who possessed immense power from Mystic, this version is significantly weaker, with a Zero to Hero type Mystic possessing limited potential, only strengthening her through intense, exhausting training.

> In return, Hoshino doesn't act impulsively or thoughtlessly, but always has a clear plan. However… it still couldn't save Yume from the immense energy from the giant worm they accidentally encountered while lost in the sandstorm.

> Yume, in protecting her, shattered the Iron Horus shield (only a single piece remained, salvaged by Hoshino's desperate efforts), and suffered severe injuries, leaving her in a vegetative state for the rest of her life. Both were lucky to survive and escape thanks to the flash grenade that Hoshino released just in time. But since then, Hoshino has had to shoulder the burden of paying for Yume's life-sustaining medical expenses and meticulously caring for her, while also struggling to pay off the school's enormous debt. That's not even mentioning the helmet gang constantly trying to rob the school, backed by the Kaisers… This made her thoughts even more extreme than ever.||

||Hoshino later sharpened a fragment from Iron Horus into a sharp axe capable of piercing even halo defenses, severing the limbs of anyone who dared to destroy her beloved school. Her personality grew increasingly gloomy, constantly blaming herself for being too weak, causing Yume to be in a vegetative state. She trained frantically, disregarding her physical limits, blindly choosing all kinds of dirty jobs just to pay off the mountain of debt and save Yume's life. (The long scar on Hoshino's face is also from the brutal battles he fought to protect the school.)
> It wasn't until Nonomi appeared that Hoshino gradually relaxed a little and took time to rest. Her appearance was like a glimmer of light shining on Hoshino's seemingly hopeless life, giving him reason to love himself and someone to share his burdens with.

> Later, as in the original story, Hoshino met Shiroko, Serika, and Ayane. But the impression the juniors had of him wasn't that of a lazy, self-centered senpai, but rather someone who was always working part-time, his face always grim like an old man due to prolonged sleep deprivation. > Regarding the event when Sensei arrived at Abydos High School, Hoshino was initially extremely wary of this strange adult, but was later completely convinced by Sensei's sincere words and actions (especially after rescuing Serika), causing her to gradually come to see Sensei as a trustworthy adult. (Unlike the original, Hoshino doesn't intensely hate adults, because she knows there are all kinds of people, and doesn't generalize at all.)
> Because there isn't a powerful Mystic like in the original universe, the target of the Black Suit is Shiroko. However, because she was discovered while following Shiroko to sign a contract, she was also captured and turned into a guinea pig for a series of inhumane experiments by the Kaisers.||

||When Sensei and everyone arrived, only Shiroko was lucky enough to be rescued, while Hoshino… her body had already ended up in a trash can, her chest devoured by crows. Sensei was forced to use his adult card, sacrificing almost his entire life to revive her by creating a new body for her soul to inhabit. (Meanwhile, Sensei cooked Hoshino's old body into various dishes to eat gradually, to avoid overlapping existences that would cause Hoshino's new body to rot.)
Bonus: Hoshino's daily life was extremely miserable because all the money she earned went to paying off debts and hospital bills, so sometimes just eating a piece of bread with jam would make her happy all day…||

||Absolute Arisu/Kei - Humanity's Last Hope
Concept: Chef Fuukari/Blade_1605
Illustration and Design: Blade_1605
- Instead of being apocalyptic princesses created by unknown priests over 1000 years ago, Arisu and Kei are twin human sisters from an ancient civilization that was wiped out over 1000 years ago by a mysterious virus of unknown origin, turning anyone who came into contact with it into metal.

- Their parents were brilliant scientists on a research team fighting the pandemic, but ultimately they were powerless due to its incredibly insidious spread. (In which Kei had been metalized to the point where only his head remained human.)
To prevent their other daughter from being infected, they led Arisu into a special containment chamber called AL1S in the basement, known only to them, which could keep her away from this world teetering on the brink of extinction. As for Kei, they transferred her consciousness into a mini-robot with supercomputer-level awareness near the chamber, as per her last wish, hoping it could continue to protect her younger sister Arisu, no matter what form she took.

- And 1000 years passed, and that civilization was completely wiped out, leaving only a desolate ruin that the student council president forbade anyone from entering.||

||- Arisu remained there, sleeping soundly inside the chamber with Kei, who had stayed awake for a thousand years just to ensure the safety of his little princess's sleep. Until the energy supply inside the basement reached its limit, Kei was forced to venture outside to find more fuel to sustain the chamber.

- Because Kei's primary form was just a versatile head that could extend wheels or robotic arms to pull objects, and with no batteries to replace, Kei could only recharge using sunlight and travel at night, making his search incredibly difficult and arduous. Therefore, to make the task easier, Kei prioritized gathering leftover mechanical parts from the ruins of the Millennium School's abandoned area, creating a more complete body for himself (this would also protect Arisu if danger arose).

- But then, inevitably, while Kei was building his new body, the special chamber ran out of battery and forcibly awakened Arisu. She awoke from 1000 years of hibernation, with a mind stuck at the age of 15.

- All memories of the world, her parents, and the plague have vanished. The only thing Arisu still remembers is her older sister, Kei (their sisterly bond is so strong that Arisu immediately recognizes her sister even when Kei is no longer human). Now, Arisu only wants to go on adventures with her sister in the world outside the basement, after hearing Kei talk about it while gathering fuel.

- However, to go outside, Arisu must always stay in the containment chamber that Kei created because her body hasn't adapted to the new world's environment yet. (Actually, Kei didn't allow it at first, but Arisu pestered her so much that she had to give in.)||
Don't know how to feel about this one

||- Waking up after 1000 years of hibernation, Kei and Arisu spend their time gathering fuel and finding more video game discs to play in the basement. Ironically, these seemingly harmless actions inadvertently create an urban legend about a mysterious robot always carrying a giant tank on its back, often wandering at night in the abandoned Millennium campus.

- One day, they encounter Sensei and the game development club being attacked by an out-of-control robot guard while on their usual adventure. Following Arisu's compassionate plea, Kei rushes to their rescue, learning that they're there to investigate the urban legend circulating about them, and quickly resolves the misunderstandings through a few simple conversations. (As for the robot's remains, Kei took them back for recycling and reuse.)
- Realizing that the two strange sisters weren't as scary as she thought, and were even fans of the game she created, Momoi quickly invited them both to join the club. Of course, they agreed, with the condition that they could only come to the club at night because they had to charge the Arisu containment chamber in the morning.||

||- Gradually, the relationship between the two sisters, the game development club, and Sensei grew stronger. Especially when everyone worked together to create an amazing RPG game that won first prize at The Millennium Awards, everyone was overjoyed.

- However… tragedy began from here. Arisu, overwhelmed with excitement, forgot that she hadn't yet adapted to the new environment and jumped out of the containment chamber to celebrate with everyone, causing the smile on her face to suddenly vanish, replaced by agonizing pain.

- Unable to stand idly by and watch her friend endure such torment and confinement, Momoi decided to devise a plan to infiltrate the restricted area – the Technical Department (which Rio had sealed off due to the dangerously high virus levels), hoping to find something that would allow Arisu to escape the containment chamber and no longer be completely dependent on Kei.

- Thanks to Sensei's skillful guidance and assistance from the Veritas group (along with the other two members of the Technical Department), the group successfully bypassed the C&C and Seminar's security, penetrating deep into the room where the disaster originated.||

||- Unfortunately, that was a terrible mistake, as no one expected that the Metalization Virus would now enter a far more terrifying stage – Mechanization (even developing its own consciousness). It transformed a student's corpse still trapped inside into a hideous mechanical monster, releasing toxic gas that easily penetrated protective armor and nearly infected everyone if not for the unexpected variable: Kei – with her special body completely immune to the virus – managed to push everyone away from the danger in time.

- After defeating the monster, the two sisters ventured inside to explore. Although they couldn't find anything outside the containment chamber that would allow Arisu to breathe normally, they did find a weapon of immense power, capable of switching between sword and gun forms, called the Light Sword (an old invention from former student council president Utaha).||

||- Meanwhile, in the fortress city of Eridu, Rio witnessed everything through mini-drones scattered throughout the Millennium. Seeing a glimmer of hope illuminating the school engulfed in disaster, Rio ordered Toki to negotiate for Kei to be brought there for research purposes, to create a protective suit (if that failed, violence would be used).

- Of course, the negotiations failed, simply because if Kei was taken away and Arisu was left behind, she would surely die, and Arisu couldn't accept her older sister being dissected, even though she also wanted to save everyone from the epidemic.

- Just as Toki was about to resort to a more extreme plan, Momoi cleverly improvised, claiming she had discovered a super-soldier suit with similar immunity in the ruins. Along with some persuasive words from Sensei, Rio, listening through Abi-Eshuh, temporarily agreed to give everyone a month to find it before the epidemic intensified.

- Momoi, Midori, Yuzu, Arisu, Kei, and Sensei began their journey into the perilous ruins, attempting to turn the lie into truth (and, in the worst-case scenario, to pave the way for the two sisters to escape from Millennium).||

||- Along the way, they gradually discovered the truth: this ruin was the home of Arisu and Kei from 1000 years ago – a civilization buried by a metal-causing plague. It turned out the virus that nearly brought Millennium to the brink of destruction came from the past, not a parallel universe. - Not only that, the group also had to face a horde of mechanical monsters with a similar origin to the one in the Technical Department (except these were people from 1000 years ago), almost pushing everyone to the brink of death.
||
